name: sprintly
description: Drive the sprintly CLI for sprintly.cloud task tracking. LLM-agnostic mirror of SKILL.md.

auth.headless_trigger: missing DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY auto-selects the code-pairing prompt
auth.logout_warning: Run only when the user explicitly asks; revokes the saved session.

lookups.returns: custom field definitions and value sets

issue show.purpose: Show full detail for one issue.

issue list.purpose: Search and list issues with rich filtering.

flags.--columns: Comma-separated columns; cf:<id> adds a custom-field column

issue list.example: sprintly issue list --assignee me --status open --sort updated_on:desc

issue create.json_semantics: JSON provides the base body; CLI flags override individual fields.

issue update.purpose: Update an existing issue and/or append a journal comment.

extra_flags.--notes: Append a journal comment without otherwise mutating the issue
extra_flags.--private-notes: Mark the appended comment as private

users.purpose: List backend users with substring / status / group filters, pagination, and selectable columns. Used to look up a numeric user id when `me` does not apply (e.g. for --watcher).

flags.--hide-admin: Client-side filter on the admin field. Backend only returns admin to admin callers; for regular users this filter is a no-op.
flags.--all: Fetch every page until total_count is reached.
flags.--columns: Comma-separated columns. Available: id,login,name,email,last-login,admin. Default: id,login,name,email,last-login.

wiki list.purpose: List wiki pages of one project.

flags.--project: Numeric project ID or project identifier (slug from `sprintly projects`)

wiki list.notes: Pagination is client-side; the wiki index endpoint does not return total_count, so there is no --all flag.

wiki show.purpose: Show a wiki page by title.

flags.--project: Numeric project ID or project identifier
flags.--title: Page name. The backend addresses wiki pages by title only; numeric IDs are not supported.
flags.--format: raw|rendered. 'rendered' is a forward-compat placeholder and currently behaves like 'raw'.

wiki show.errors: Missing title returns a clear not-found error via describeBackendError; spaces in title are URL-encoded automatically.

wiki edit.purpose: Upsert a wiki page by title — one command for both create and update.

flags.--project: Numeric project ID or project identifier
flags.--title: Page name (primary key for wiki pages)
flags.--text: Inline body. Mutually exclusive with --text-file and --text-stdin.
flags.--text-file: Read body from file. Mutually exclusive with --text and --text-stdin.
flags.--text-stdin: Read body from stdin. Mutually exclusive with --text and --text-file.
flags.--parent: Parent page title (sets parent_title on the page)
flags.--comments: Revision note attached to this edit

wiki edit.notes: PUT without text is valid when you only want to change --parent or attach --comments to an existing page. Body follows the same primitive-HTML rule as --description/--notes.
wiki edit.example:
sprintly wiki edit --project spr --title 'Release notes' --text '

Shipped #54.

' --comments 'ship note'

text_format.rule: Human-readable text wrapped in primitive HTML tags. Plain unmarked text and Markdown are both wrong: Markdown (**bold**, # heading, backticks, - list) renders as raw syntax in the web UI.

story_workflow.rule: When implementing a story that has sub-tasks, keep the sprintly tracker in sync at all times. The in-session task list is invisible to the user later; only journal notes and statuses on sub-tasks survive.

per_sub_task.action: Transition to in progress (status 2; verify with `sprintly statuses`).
If you hit a blocker, scope change, or split a sub-task, leave a --notes entry explaining why. Do not rely on memory or the in-session task list.
Transition to Done (status 3) AND post a short --notes summary: files/functions touched, decisions made, verification results. Do not paste diffs.
per_sub_task.example:
sprintly issue update --id 41 --status 3 --notes "

Added User in Cli.Backend; admin is Maybe Bool so the client-side filter degrades cleanly.

"

story_workflow.parent_story: When the story itself is finished, post a summary note on the parent issue, but do not auto-transition the parent unless the user explicitly asks. Let them choose review/testing/done.
story_workflow.why: The sprintly journal is the only artefact the user can read tomorrow to understand what shipped and why. Skipping it leaves the board lying — sub-tasks stuck in `to do` while their code is merged.

known_gaps.summary: There is no issue delete command.
known_gaps.workaround: Close the issue by setting --status to the Done ID from `sprintly statuses`.
Use `issue update --id N --notes "…"` to add a journal entry.
